questions.question: Which component of Windows OS allows users to modify system settings such as display resolution, sound preferences and user accounts?

What does PCB stand for in electronics?
In the context of file deletion, what is the typical immediate effect of moving a file to the 'Trash' or 'Recycle Bin' in a GUI-based Operating System?
questions.option_1: The file is moved to a special directory, and its directory entry is updated, but the data remains on the storage device.
questions.option_2: The file is compressed and archived before being permanently deleted.
questions.option_3: The file's metadata (inode or equivalent) is immediately purged from the file system.
questions.option_4: The file's data is immediately overwritten and unrecoverable.

questions.question: Which of the following methods is the easiest way to sort files by name in File Explorer in Windows 11 OS?
questions.option_1: Drag files into alphabetical order manually
questions.option_2: Click the Name column header in File Explorer

questions.question: Which of the following characters is NOT allowed in a filename when renaming in Windows?
Which feature in Windows allows you to create shortcuts to frequently used folders for easier access?
Which feature in Windows allows you to recover files that were accidentally deleted?
Which of the following actions will create a new folder on a Windows desktop?
questions.option_1: Clicking on the Recycle Bin icon
questions.option_2: Right-clicking on the desktop and selecting 'New' → 'Folder'

questions.question: Which desktop element in Windows OS allows users to restore deleted files and folders?
Which of the following is the most effective method for organizing files and folders in a computer's operating system?
questions.option_1: Creating a logical folder structure that reflects the content, using clear, descriptive names
questions.option_2: Renaming folders frequently to prevent them from becoming cluttered
questions.option_3: Naming files and folders with random characters to avoid confusion
questions.option_4: Storing all files and folders in one directory to save space

questions.question: Which of the following conditions will prevent a file from being renamed in a Windows-based operating system?
questions.option_1: The file is currently opened in another application.
questions.option_2: The file has a long name exceeding 255 characters.
questions.option_3: The file is located in the Recycle Bin.
questions.option_4: The file has the 'Read-only' attribute enabled.

questions.question: Which method allows you to create a new text file inside a specific folder in Windows?
questions.option_1: Open the folder and right-click → New → Text Document
questions.option_2: Start a new task from Task Manager.
questions.option_3: Restore a file from the Recycle Bin.
questions.option_4: Press Ctrl + F while in the folder.

questions.question: In the context of file systems, what is the primary purpose of a file system 'metadata'?
questions.option_1: To store the actual content and data of the files
questions.option_2: To define the graphical representation of files and folders for the user
questions.option_3: To optimise the physical storage of file data on the storage device for faster access
questions.option_4: To record information about the files and directories, such as their names, sizes, timestamps and permissions

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using a Directory Acyclic Graph (DAG) structure for file systems compared to a simple tree structure?
questions.option_1: DAGs reduce disk fragmentation and improve storage efficiency.
questions.option_2: DAGs allow for faster traversal and searching of files.
questions.option_3: DAGs inherently provide better data security and access control.
questions.option_4: DAGs enable the creation of hard links, allowing a single file to have multiple directory entries.
